question: Show ids for orders including both "Pride and Prejudice" and "The Little Prince".
gold_sql: SELECT idOrder FROM Book AS T1 JOIN Books_Order AS T2 ON T1.isbn  =  T2.isbn WHERE T1.title  =  "Pride and Prejudice" INTERSECT SELECT idOrder FROM Book AS T1 JOIN Books_Order AS T2 ON T1.isbn  =  T2.isbn WHERE T1.title  =  "The Little Prince"
predicted_sql: SELECT Orders.IdOrder FROM Orders JOIN Author_Book ON Orders.IdClient = Author_Book.IdAuthor JOIN Author ON Author.idAuthor = Author_Book.Author JOIN Books_Order ON Books_Order.ISBN = Book.ISBN JOIN Book ON Book.ISBN = Books_Order.ISBN WHERE Book.Title IN ('Pride and Prejudice', 'The Little Prince')

question: What are the names of the staff members who have been both a speaker and a sponsor at some conference?
gold_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM staff AS T1 JOIN conference_participation AS T2 ON T1.staff_id  =  T2.staff_id WHERE T2.role  =  'Speaker' INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM staff AS T1 JOIN conference_participation AS T2 ON T1.staff_id  =  T2.staff_id WHERE T2.role  =  'Sponsor'
predicted_sql: SELECT staff.name FROM staff JOIN conference_participation ON staff.staff_ID = conference_participation.staff_ID JOIN institution ON staff.Institution_ID = institution.Institution_ID WHERE role = 'speaker' AND role = 'sponsor'

question: What are the 10 most cited papers, and how many citations did each have?
gold_sql: SELECT paper_id ,  count(*) FROM Citation GROUP BY cited_paper_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 10
predicted_sql: SELECT Paper.title, COUNT(Citation.paper_id) AS citations FROM Paper JOIN Citation ON Paper.paper_id = Citation.paper_id GROUP BY Paper.title, Citation.paper_id ORDER BY citations DESC LIMIT 10
predicted_answer: Supervised Noun Phrase Coreference Research: The First Fifteen Years
Generating Phrasal and Sentential Paraphrases: A Survey of Data-Driven Methods
Overview of the SPMRL 2013 Shared Task: A Cross-Framework Evaluation of Parsing Morphologically Rich Languages
Findings of the 2013 Workshop on Statistical Machine Translation
Computational Generation of Referring Expressions: A Survey
Introduction To The Special Issue On Word Sense Disambiguation: The State Of The Art
Wide-Coverage Efficient Statistical Parsing with CCG and Log-Linear Models
Learning Sentential Paraphrases from Bilingual Parallel Corpora for Text-to-Text Generation

question: What is the name of the institution with no staff in the records?
gold_sql: SELECT institution_name FROM institution WHERE institution_id NOT IN (SELECT institution_id FROM staff)
predicted_sql: SELECT institution.Institution_Name FROM institution JOIN staff ON institution.Institution_ID = staff.Institution_ID WHERE staff.staff_ID IS NULL

question: How many different types of contents are stored in each warehouse?
gold_sql: SELECT count(DISTINCT CONTENTS) ,  warehouse FROM boxes GROUP BY warehouse
predicted_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT Contents) FROM Boxes

question: Find the countries where no driver come from.
gold_sql: SELECT country FROM country WHERE country_id NOT IN (SELECT country FROM driver)
predicted_sql: SELECT Country FROM country EXCEPT SELECT Country FROM driver

question: What are the names of distinct racing bikes that are purchased by the cyclists with better results than '4:21.558' ?
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.product_name FROM cyclist AS T1 JOIN cyclists_own_bikes AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.cyclist_id JOIN bike AS T3 ON T2.bike_id  =  T3.id WHERE T1.result  <  '4:21.558'
predicted_sql: SELECT cyclist.name FROM cyclists_own_bikes JOIN cyclists ON cyclists_own_bikes.cyclist_id = cyclists.id WHERE cyclists.result > '4:21.558'
gold_answer: CANNONDALE SUPERSIX EVO HI-MOD DURA ACE

question: How many reservations exist for each boat that has more than 1 reservation already?
gold_sql: SELECT bid ,  count(*) FROM Reserves GROUP BY bid HAVING count(*)  >  1
predicted_sql: SELECT Sid, COUNT(*) AS Reservations FROM Reserves GROUP BY Sid

question: What are the titles of books with sale prices above the average sale price across all books?
gold_sql: SELECT title FROM book WHERE saleprice  >  (SELECT avg(saleprice) FROM book)
predicted_sql: SELECT Title FROM Books ORDER BY SalePrice ASC LIMIT 1

question: Show all client ids and the number of invoices for each client.
gold_sql: SELECT client_id ,  count(*) FROM Invoices GROUP BY client_id
predicted_sql: SELECT Clients.client_id, COUNT(Invoices.invoice_id) AS invoice_count FROM Clients JOIN Invoices ON Clients.agency_id = Invoices.client_id GROUP BY Clients.client_id

question: Show the number of documents.
gold_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Documents
predicted_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Documents

question: Which customers used the least commonly-used service ? Give me the distinct customer details .
gold_sql: select distinct t1.customer_details from customers as t1 join customers_and_services as t2 on t1.customer_id  =  t2.customer_id where t2.service_id  =  (select service_id from services group by service_id order by count(*) asc limit 1)
predicted_sql: SELECT Customers.Customer_Details FROM Customers JOIN Customers_and_Services ON Customers.Customer_ID = Customers_and_Services.Customer_ID JOIN Services ON Customers_and_Services.Service_ID = Services.Service_ID WHERE Services.Service_Details = (SELECT Service_Details FROM Services ORDER BY Service_Details ASC LIMIT 1) AND Customers.Customer_Details IS NOT NULL

question: Count the number of drivers who have not driven any vehicles.
gold_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM driver WHERE driver_id NOT IN ( SELECT driver_id FROM vehicle_driver )
predicted_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT driver.Driver_ID) FROM driver

question: List the detail and id of the teacher who teaches the most courses.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.teacher_details ,  T1.teacher_id FROM Teachers AS T1 JOIN Classes AS T2 ON T1.teacher_id  =  T2.teacher_id GROUP BY T1.teacher_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
predicted_sql: SELECT Students.student_id, Teachers.teacher_id FROM Students JOIN Teachers ON Students.student_id = Teachers.teacher_id GROUP BY Teachers.teacher_id ORDER BY COUNT(Classes.class_id) DESC LIMIT 1

question: Show different nationalities of customers, along with the number of customers of each nationality.
gold_sql: SELECT Nationality ,  COUNT(*) FROM customer GROUP BY Nationality
predicted_sql: SELECT Nationality, COUNT(*) AS Number_of_Customers FROM customer GROUP BY Nationality

question: Show the names of club leaders that joined their club before 2018.
gold_sql: SELECT T3.Name ,  T2.Club_Name FROM club_leader AS T1 JOIN club AS T2 ON T1.Club_ID  =  T2.Club_ID JOIN member AS T3 ON T1.Member_ID  =  T3.Member_ID WHERE T1.Year_Join  <  2018
predicted_sql: SELECT club_leader.Member_ID, club_leader.Club_ID, club_leader.Year_Join FROM club_leader JOIN club ON club_leader.Club_ID = club.Club_ID WHERE club.Year_Join < 2018

question: Find the code and content of all boxes whose value is higher than the value of all boxes with Scissors as content.
gold_sql: SELECT code ,  CONTENTS FROM boxes WHERE value  >  (SELECT max(value) FROM boxes WHERE CONTENTS  =  'Scissors')
predicted_sql: SELECT Boxes.Code, Boxes.Contents FROM Boxes WHERE Boxes.Value > (SELECT Value FROM Boxes WHERE Boxes.Contents = 'Scissors')

question: What are the names of all Hardware products, sorted by price ascending?
gold_sql: SELECT product_name FROM Products WHERE product_type_code  =  "Hardware" ORDER BY product_price ASC
predicted_sql: SELECT Customers.customer_name FROM Customers JOIN Products ON Customers.customer_id = Products.product_id ORDER BY Products.product_price ASC

question: What are the ids, flavors, and food types of goods, ordered by price?
gold_sql: SELECT id ,  flavor ,  food FROM goods ORDER BY price
predicted_sql: SELECT Receipt, Flavor, Food FROM goods JOIN items ON items.Item = goods.Id ORDER BY Price

question: Which customer shopped most often? How many times?
gold_sql: SELECT CustomerId ,  count(*) FROM receipts GROUP BY CustomerId ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
predicted_sql: SELECT customers.LastName, COUNT(receipts.ReceiptNumber) AS total_shops FROM customers JOIN receipts ON customers.Id = receipts.CustomerId GROUP BY customers.LastName

question: Which packages have weight between 10 and 30? List the package number and weight.
gold_sql: SELECT PackageNumber ,  Weight FROM PACKAGE WHERE Weight BETWEEN 10 AND 30;
predicted_sql: SELECT Package.PackageNumber, Package.Weight FROM Package WHERE Package.Weight BETWEEN 10 AND 30

question: What is the title of the paper that has most number of authors?
gold_sql: SELECT T2.title FROM Author_list AS T1 JOIN Paper AS T2 ON T1.paper_id  =  T2.paper_id GROUP BY T2.paper_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
predicted_sql: SELECT Paper.title FROM Paper JOIN Author_list ON Paper.paper_id = Author_list.paper_id JOIN Author ON Author.author_id = Author_list.author_id WHERE Author.name = (SELECT MAX(name) FROM Author)

question: WHat are the first and last names, and middle initials of all customers who did not make any orders?
gold_sql: SELECT customer_first_name ,  customer_middle_initial ,  customer_last_name FROM Customers EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_first_name ,  T1.customer_middle_initial ,  T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id  =  T2.customer_id
predicted_sql: SELECT Customers.customer_first_name, Customers.customer_middle_initial FROM Customers WHERE Customers.customer_id NOT IN (SELECT Customers.customer_id FROM Orders JOIN Order_Items ON Orders.order_id = Order_Items.order_id JOIN Products ON Order_Items.product_id = Products.product_id WHERE Orders.order_status_code = 'Completed' OR Orders.order_status_code = 'Cancelled' OR Orders.order_status_code = 'Shipped' OR Orders.order_status_code = 'Returned' OR Orders.order_status_code = 'Cancel' OR Orders.order_status_code = 'Cancel Order')

question: How are all the achievements described? List the achievement detail and the type description.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.achievement_details ,  T2.achievement_type_description FROM Achievements AS T1 JOIN Ref_Achievement_Type AS T2 ON T1.achievement_type_code  =  T2.achievement_type_code
predicted_sql: SELECT achievement_type_description, achievement_details FROM Ref_Achievement_Type JOIN Achievements ON Achievements.achievement_type_code = Ref_Achievement_Type.achievement_type_code

question: What are the names of the conferences that have the top 2 most people attending?
gold_sql: SELECT T1.conference_name FROM Conference AS T1 JOIN Conference_participation AS T2 ON T1.conference_id  =  T2.conference_id GROUP BY T2.conference_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 2
predicted_sql: SELECT conference.Conference_Name, institution.Institution_Name FROM conference JOIN institution ON conference.Conference_ID = institution.Institution_ID ORDER BY Event_Attendance DESC LIMIT 2

question: What is the title and location of the oldest painting ?
gold_sql: select title ,  location from paintings order by year limit 1
predicted_sql: SELECT title, location FROM Paintings JOIN Artists ON Paintings.painterID = Artists.artistID WHERE year = (SELECT MAX(year) FROM Paintings)

question: What are the ranks and names of all majors at Augustana College?
gold_sql: SELECT T1.rank ,  T3.major_name FROM Major_Ranking AS T1 JOIN University AS T2 JOIN Major AS T3 ON T1.university_id  =  T2.university_id AND T1.major_id  =  T3.major_id WHERE T2.university_name  =  'Augustana College'
predicted_sql: SELECT university.University_Name, major_ranking.Rank FROM university INNER JOIN major_ranking ON university.University_ID = major_ranking.University_ID

question: List the names of members whose nationality is not `` England '' .
gold_sql: select name from member where nationality != "england"
predicted_sql: SELECT Name FROM member WHERE Nationality != 'England'

question: What is the total weight for all packages that Leo Wong sent?

question: What is the process outcome description and process status description for the document with id 0?
gold_sql: SELECT T2.process_outcome_description ,  T3.process_status_description FROM Documents_processes AS T1 JOIN Process_outcomes AS T2 ON T1.process_outcome_code  =  T2.process_outcome_code JOIN Process_Status AS T3 ON T1.process_status_code  =  T3.process_status_code WHERE T1.document_id  =  0
predicted_sql: SELECT Process_Outcomes.process_outcome_description, Process_Status.process_status_description FROM Process_Outcomes JOIN Process_Status ON Process_Outcomes.process_outcome_code = Process_Status.process_status_code WHERE Process_Outcomes.document_id = 0

question: List id of documents that in collection named Best but not in document subset Best for 2000.
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.Document_Object_ID FROM Collections AS T1 JOIN Documents_in_Collections AS T2 ON T1.Collection_ID = T2.Collection_ID WHERE T1.Collection_Name = "Best" EXCEPT SELECT DISTINCT T3.Document_Object_ID FROM Document_Subset_Members AS T3 JOIN Document_Subsets AS T4 ON T3.Document_Subset_ID =  T4.Document_Subset_ID WHERE T4.Document_Subset_Name = "Best for 2000"
predicted_sql: SELECT Document_Object_ID FROM Documents_in_Collections WHERE Collection_ID = (SELECT Collection_ID FROM Collections WHERE Collection_Name = 'Best') AND Document_Object_ID NOT IN (SELECT Document_Object_ID FROM Document_Objects WHERE Document_Subset_ID = (SELECT Document_Subset_ID FROM Document_Subsets WHERE Document_Subset_Name = 'Best' AND Year = 2000))

question: Show all agency ids and details for agencies with a client.

predicted_sql: SELECT Agencies.agency_id, Agencies.agency_details FROM Agencies JOIN Clients ON Agencies.agency_id = Clients.agency_id WHERE Clients.client_id IS NOT NULL

question: Find the name and powertrain type of the cars that rented for more than 30 total hours.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.name ,  T2.Type_of_powertrain FROM renting_history AS T1 JOIN vehicles AS T2 ON T1.vehicles_id  =  T2.id GROUP BY T1.vehicles_id HAVING sum(T1.total_hours)  >  30
predicted_sql: SELECT Customers.name, Vehicles.Type_of_powertrain FROM Customers JOIN Renting_history ON Customers.id = Renting_history.customer_id JOIN Vehicles ON Renting_history.vehicles_id = Vehicles.id WHERE Renting_history.total_hours > 30

question: In which years did both buildings with more than 20 stories and buildings with less than 15 stories were completed?
gold_sql: SELECT Completed_Year FROM building WHERE Number_of_Stories  >  20 INTERSECT SELECT Completed_Year FROM building WHERE Number_of_Stories  <  15
predicted_sql: SELECT Completed_Year FROM building WHERE Number_of_Stories > 20 AND Building_ID IN (SELECT Building_ID FROM building WHERE Number_of_Stories < 15)

question: Sort all the books in descending order of release date, and return the book titles.
gold_sql: SELECT title FROM book ORDER BY release_date DESC
predicted_sql: SELECT Title FROM book ORDER BY Release_date DESC

predicted_sql: SELECT DISTINCT payment_method_code FROM Customers

question: What are the names of all players in alphabetical order?
gold_sql: SELECT Player_name FROM player ORDER BY Player_name ASC
predicted_sql: SELECT player.Player_name FROM player ORDER BY player.Player_name

question: Who is the owner of the parent document of every documents where 'Marlin' is the owner?
gold_sql: SELECT T2.Owner FROM Document_Objects AS T1 JOIN Document_Objects AS T2 ON T1.Parent_Document_Object_ID = T2.Document_Object_ID WHERE T1.Owner  =  'Marlin'
predicted_sql: SELECT Owner FROM Document_Objects WHERE Owner = 'Marlin'

question: Show all names who have been in both ACL and Naccl.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.name FROM staff AS T1 JOIN conference_participation AS T2 JOIN Conference AS T3 ON T1.staff_id  =  T2.staff_id AND T2.conference_id  =  T3.conference_id WHERE T3.Conference_name  =  'ACL' INTERSECT SELECT T1.name FROM staff AS T1 JOIN conference_participation AS T2 JOIN conference AS T3 ON T1.staff_id  =  T2.staff_id AND T2.conference_id  =  T3.conference_id WHERE T3.Conference_name  =  'Naccl'
predicted_sql: SELECT name FROM staff WHERE Institution_ID IN (SELECT Institution_ID FROM institution WHERE Founded = 1999) AND staff_ID IN (SELECT staff_ID FROM conference_participation WHERE role = 'ACL' AND Conference_ID IN (SELECT Conference_ID FROM conference WHERE Location = 'Naccl'))

question: Select the warehouse code and the average value of the boxes only for those warehouses where the average value of the boxes is greater than 150.
gold_sql: SELECT warehouse ,  avg(value) FROM boxes GROUP BY warehouse HAVING avg(value)  >  150
predicted_sql: SELECT Warehouses.Code, (SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes WHERE Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code) AS average_value FROM Warehouses WHERE (SELECT AVG(Boxes.Value) FROM Boxes WHERE Boxes.Warehouse = Warehouses.Code) > 150

question: Find the name and id of Sailors (sid) that reserved red and blue boat.
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.sid ,  T3.name FROM Boats AS T1 JOIN Reserves AS T2 ON  T1.bid  =  T2.bid JOIN Sailors AS T3 ON T2.sid  =  T3.sid WHERE T1.color  =  'red' INTERSECT SELECT DISTINCT T2.sid ,  T3.name FROM Boats AS T1 JOIN Reserves AS T2 ON  T1.bid  =  T2.bid JOIN Sailors AS T3 ON T2.sid  =  T3.sid WHERE T1.color  =  "blue"
predicted_sql: SELECT Sailors.name, Sailors.sid FROM Sailors JOIN Reserves ON Reserves.sid = Sailors.sid WHERE Reserves.bid = (SELECT bid FROM Boats WHERE color = 'red' OR color = 'blue')

question: What are the distinct ids of all goods that are cheaper than some goods of type Tart?
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT id FROM goods WHERE price  <  (SELECT max(price) FROM goods WHERE food  =  "Tart")
predicted_sql: SELECT DISTINCT goods.Id FROM goods JOIN items ON goods.Id = items.Receipt WHERE items.Food = 'Tart'

question: What are the names of all universities in ascending order of reputation points?
gold_sql: SELECT T1.university_name FROM University AS T1 JOIN Overall_ranking AS T2 ON T1.university_id  =  T2.university_id ORDER BY T2.reputation_point
predicted_sql: SELECT university.University_Name FROM university ORDER BY university.Affiliation ASC

question: Show the agency ids and the number of staff in each agent?

predicted_sql: SELECT Agencies.agency_id, COUNT(Staff.staff_id) AS number_of_staff FROM Agencies JOIN Staff ON Agencies.agency_id = Staff.agency_id GROUP BY Agencies.agency_id

question: Which student has the loan with the minimum value? List the student's biographical information.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.bio_data FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Loans AS T2 ON T1.student_id  =  T2.student_id ORDER BY T2.amount_of_loan ASC LIMIT 1
predicted_sql: SELECT student_id, bio_data, student_details FROM Students WHERE student_id = (SELECT student_id FROM Student_Loans ORDER BY amount_of_loan ASC LIMIT 1)

question: Find the name and number of citations of the author who has most citations among all authors?
gold_sql: SELECT T3.name ,  count(*) FROM Citation AS T1 JOIN Author_list AS T2 ON T1.cited_paper_id  =  T2.paper_id JOIN Author AS T3 ON T2.author_id  =  T3.author_id GROUP BY T2.author_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
predicted_sql: SELECT Author.name, COUNT(Citation.paper_id) AS citations FROM Author JOIN Citation ON Author.author_id = Citation.paper_id GROUP BY Author.author_id ORDER BY citations DESC LIMIT 1

question: Which headphone model has the highest price?
gold_sql: SELECT model FROM headphone ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 1
predicted_sql: SELECT Model, Price FROM headphone ORDER BY Price DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many products are there for each product type?
gold_sql: SELECT product_type_code ,  count(*) FROM Products GROUP BY product_type_code
predicted_sql: SELECT product_type_code, COUNT(*) AS count FROM Products GROUP BY product_type_code

question: Show the average overall scores of countries whose official language is "English".
gold_sql: SELECT avg(T1.overall_score) FROM countries AS T1 JOIN official_languages AS T2 ON T1.id  =  T2.country_id JOIN languages AS T3 ON T2.language_id  =  T3.id WHERE T3.name  =  "English"
predicted_sql: SELECT countries.overall_score AS average_score FROM countries JOIN official_languages ON countries.id = official_languages.country_id WHERE official_languages.language_id = (SELECT id FROM languages WHERE name = 'English')
